title: ABA Services Website Design and Development

client.signals: Seeking professional website, mentions 'visually appealing' and 'user-friendly', values both design and development, intermediate budget consciousness, U.S.-based operation

summary: An ABA (Applied Behavior Analysis) clinic needs a professional website to present their services, values, and expertise to potential clients and families. They want design and development as a unified package, with emphasis on clear communication of services and modern aesthetics.

explicit_requirements.text: Design and develop a professional website for ABA services
Create a visually appealing design that communicates services and values
Integrate web design and web development seamlessly from concept to launch

implicit_needs: Trust-building visuals and messaging (healthcare-adjacent industry, serving families with children with autism)
Clear services/offerings page (what types of therapy, age ranges, specialties)
Contact or intake form for prospect inquiries
Team/staff bios (testimonials, credentials, expertise to build confidence)
About/values page explaining the clinic's philosophy
Professional imagery or placeholder imagery of a welcoming clinic environment
Fast load times and accessibility (serves families with diverse needs)
SEO basics (local visibility for ABA services in their area)
HIPAA-adjacent awareness (privacy-conscious content, no exposed client data)
domain: Applied Behavior Analysis (ABA) Services / Healthcare-Adjacent Web Presence
tech_hints: No stack specified by client; opportunity to recommend modern, maintainable approach
Consider: Next.js/React for fast SSR, static hosting (Vercel/Netlify) for simple scaling
Or: WordPress with professional theme if client prefers CMS + low maintenance
success_signals: Clean, modern, professional aesthetic that conveys trust and expertise
Fast page load times and responsive design across all devices
Clear visual hierarchy and intuitive navigation
Compelling services overview that differentiates the clinic
Live demo shows realistic ABA clinic site structure (hero, services, team, testimonial, intake, footer)
application_questions: What frameworks have you worked with?
Describe your recent experience with similar projects

budget.notes: Project is marked 'Ongoing', suggesting this may be phase 1 (MVP) with potential for follow-up work. $500 is well below market rate for full design + dev. Likely scope is 5-7 page brochure site. Implies use of templates, themes, or rapid scaffolding; custom bespoke design unlikely at this budget.

red_flags: Budget ($500) is significantly below market rate for both design and development of a professional website (typical: $2k-$8k+). Scope may need aggressive scoping or candidate must be willing to undercharge for portfolio/ongoing relationship.
No specifics on required pages, features, or design preferences. Vague scope ('visually appealing', 'user-friendly' are not measurable).
No brand assets, color scheme, or competitor examples provided. Designer will need to lead the vision.
Location restriction (U.S. only) may be cost-driven (avoiding cheaper offshore), but limits talent pool.
No mention of backend requirements (email, CMS, dynamic content), so it's unclear if this is a static brochure site or if there are hidden complexity layers.
'Ongoing project' is ambiguous. Could mean retention work post-launch or could be a red flag for perpetual scope creep without additional payment.

demo_concepts.name: ABA Clinic Website Showcase
demo_concepts.what: A fully functional, mock ABA clinic website with hero section, services overview, team bios, testimonial, contact form, and footer. All data is hardcoded/mock; no backend required.
demo_concepts.hero_feature: Compelling hero banner with clinic tagline, call-to-action button, and welcoming imagery (stock or placeholder) that sets a professional, family-friendly tone.
demo_concepts.why_wins: Demonstrates the exact deliverable the client expects (a complete, modern clinic website) with no backend complexity. Shows mastery of layout, responsive design, typography, color psychology, and conversion-focused CTAs. Proves the candidate understands ABA clinic brand positioning.

demo_concepts.name: Interactive Services Explorer
demo_concepts.what: A single-page component that lists ABA services (e.g., In-Home Therapy, Center-Based Services, Parent Training, School Consultation) with expandable details, age ranges, and 'Learn More' links. Mock data only.
demo_concepts.hero_feature: Smooth expand/collapse animations and category filtering (by age group, service type) that makes browsing services intuitive and delightful.
demo_concepts.why_wins: Shows the candidate can build interactive UI components that make healthcare services easy to explore. Demonstrates UX thinking beyond static pages. Clinics often need to list multiple service tiers; this proves the candidate can present complex information clearly.

demo_concepts.name: Contact/Intake Form with Validation
demo_concepts.what: A professional intake form (name, email, phone, child's age, service interest, message) with real-time validation, error states, and a success confirmation page. Form data is logged to console (no backend).
demo_concepts.hero_feature: Clean, accessible form design with helpful field descriptions, smart validation messages, and a friendly confirmation screen that reassures the user their message was received.
demo_concepts.why_wins: Intake/contact is the #1 conversion tool for a services clinic. Shows the candidate understands healthcare form best practices (privacy messaging, clear instructions, inclusive language). Proves ability to deliver a core business tool.
